depression.description: I often find myself feeling overwhelmed by sadness when I think about my brother's death, and I don't know how to process this grief.
I feel like I'm watching my life from a distance, unable to fully connect with my feelings or experiences, which makes everything seem dull and unimportant.
I worry that if I express my feelings about my brother, I will bring everyone down, so I often keep my emotions to myself.
Whenever I try to talk about my emotions, I feel like I can't find the right words, and it just makes me feel more isolated.
I feel a lot of tension with my family because we never talk about our pain, and it makes me feel even more alone in my grief.

Delusion.description: I often think that I'm unlovable and that my family will reject me if I show any vulnerability or weakness.
I feel like being sensitive about my brother's death makes me weak, and I hate that I can't seem to just move on like everyone else.
